acceptance.proof: The Rust 1.80 Clippy identity and normalized 319-library/350-all-target diagnostic baseline are exact, canonical, machine-independent, and reject missing, changed, reordered, duplicated, and new diagnostics.
The installed pinned Rust 1.80 Clippy emits exactly the accepted normalized baseline; toolchain/component drift and any diagnostic drift fail closed.
The existing durable LSLC-003J live closure and additive LSLC-003K pinned lint policy both run through v2 current dispatch while all historical evidence remains unchanged.
Ordinary tests and public/workflow boundaries pass with no production Rust, public API, protocol, feature activation, dependency, device, or authority changes.

commit_policy: Commit proposal and lifecycle separately, then one additive baseline/checker/test/CI/documentation family, validation, acceptance, and no-force publication evidence; preserve all historical bytes and existing warning debt.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Route the pinned Rust 1.80 Clippy baseline policy and its no-cleanup boundary.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Run focused, current-dispatch, boundary, and workflow gates.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Name the pinned lint policy in the contributor validation path.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Run focused checker and CI routing tests.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Document normalization, exact comparison, damaged cases, toolchain pin, and baseline-update authority.
Review validation ownership, private recovery routing, and public boundary; installed guidance already covers this validator-only unit.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Reviewed installed skill and corrected owner workflow.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Review evidence-role separation, fail-closed baseline policy, and validation tiers; installed guidance already covers this unit.

non_scope: Broad Clippy warning cleanup, suppression attributes, lint-level changes, or production Rust edits.
Public API, runtime behavior, protocol bytes, feature descriptors or lock, dependencies, devices, external implementation intake, adapters, or Manifold authority.
Editing the v1 manifest, any historical checker bytes, historical receipts, or LSLC-003I/003J preserved history.
objective: Establish a machine-independent exact baseline for the existing pinned Rust 1.80 Clippy diagnostic set and fail closed on toolchain or diagnostic drift without cleaning up or suppressing existing warnings.
outputs: A canonical baseline records the exact normalized 319 library warnings and 350 all-target test warnings under Rust 1.80 Clippy.
A focused checker verifies pinned tool identity and exact diagnostic multiset equality; damaged fixtures cover missing, changed, duplicate, reordered, new, and wrong-toolchain cases.
CI and v2 current dispatch execute the policy while the durable LSLC-003J live closure remains current and historical evidence stays byte-identical.
